reason_for_decision: The article is conceptually clear, but it does not yet meet the strict pass threshold because the body is too short and the explanation needs more operational depth around how RCS is used and interpreted.
major_issues: The article is below the strict 1000-word pass floor.
It needs more operational depth on why published RCS figures are conditional and how engineers use RCS in planning.
The current version explains the concept well for beginners but stops short of enough decision-oriented depth for the strict pass bar.
revision_actions: Expand the article to exceed 1000 words while keeping the beginner-friendly tone.
Add sections on why RCS values are shorthand, how viewpoint and measurement assumptions matter, and how RCS interacts with detection-range planning.
Strengthen the practical explanation of what low-RCS does and does not mean in real radar operations.
strengths: The article already explains clearly that RCS is not the same as physical size.
The core beginner framing is readable and technically responsible.
The sections on aspect angle and why RCS is not one fixed number provide a good foundation.
summary_assessment: This article has a solid conceptual base, but it needs a fuller treatment to pass the stricter workflow. The right next step is expansion and sharpening, not a full rewrite.
